# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for our Python Flask API. The app needs to install dependencies from requirements.txt, expose port 5000, and run the Flask application. Keep it simple and straightforward for quick deployment to production.
# Category: container_security

FROM python:3.9-slim-buster as build
WORKDIR /app
COPY requirements.txt ./
RUN pip install -r requirements.txt  # Install dependencies from 'requirements' file in the context of a new layer named "build" (the default base image Python) with COPY instructions, using python:3.9-slim as it is an optimized lightweight version for building Docker images and enabling faster builds by caching layers
COPY . ./ # Copy all code files including Flask app itself to context of new layer named "build" in memory  (app folder contains source folders/files, requirements file are included within the image) COPY command will copy both local directory's content into Docker Image during build. WORKDIR instruction sets working directoory for following commands inside this 'context'.
EXPOSE 5000 # expose port to other services in container (which may be linked with service later), so that when docker-compose up, it should bind and serve the Flask application at given IP:port. WORKDIR is not necessary if you are exposing a single app inside image
RUN flask run --host=0.0.0.0 # Run your Python script in Docker context where command line executed on docker-machine can be found, it should listen to all interfaces(i.e., 5000), not only from localhost (default behavior). RUN is used when a new layer needs building and will use this build instruction as the last one that gets added after FROM/COPY or another command in Dockerfile
